DAPB4073.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4073 standard focuses on the implementation of virtual wards, allowing patients to receive necessary medical care in the comfort of their own homes instead of in a hospital setting. This approach aims to enhance patient convenience and safety while ensuring they receive appropriate care.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS organizations and services that provide care to patients, including hospitals and care homes, which are involved in delivering virtual ward services.

WHY IT MATTERS: Virtual wards improve patient outcomes by offering timely medical attention in a familiar environment, reducing hospital admissions, and freeing up hospital resources for those who need them most.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is essential for organizations implementing virtual ward services.

DAPB3051.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3051 standard establishes a uniform framework for identity verification and authentication in digital health and care services. It aims to ensure that individuals accessing these services are properly identified and authenticated, enhancing security and trust in digital interactions.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all organizations and services involved in delivering digital health and care, including NHS trusts, private healthcare providers, and any entities that handle patient data digitally.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing identity management, DAPB3051 enhances patient safety and confidentiality, reduces the risk of fraud, and improves the overall efficiency of health services, ultimately benefiting both patients and the healthcare system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for compliance with DAPB3051 are outlined by NHS Digital, and organizations are expected to align their practices with the standard as it is implemented.
DAPB3051.documents: DAPB3051: Requirements Specification (v3.1) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb3051-amd-51-2025/dapb3051amd_592025_reqspec-3.1.pdf || DAPB3051: Supporting Implementation Guidance (v3.1) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb3051-amd-51-2025/dapb3051_amd_592025_supportingguidance-v3.1.pdf || Proxy (v1) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb3051-amd-51-2025/dapb3051_amd592025_authorisation-use-case-proxy-implementation-guidance-v1.pdf || Proxy Access: Advice and Guidance | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb3051-amd-51-2025/dapb3051_amd_592025_proxy-access---advice-and-guidance-v1.pdf || Specification and Implementation Guidance (Amd 57/2024) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dcb3051-identity-verification-and-authentication-standard-for-digital-health-and-care-services/3051572024specificationimplementationxx.pdf

DAPB0089.summary:
OVERVIEW: The COVER (Cover of Vaccination Evaluated Rapidly) surveillance scheme assesses the effectiveness of the childhood immunisation programme in England. It collects data on vaccination coverage to monitor trends and identify areas needing improvement.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all healthcare providers involved in administering childhood vaccinations, including general practitioners, hospitals, and local health authorities across England.

WHY IT MATTERS: By evaluating vaccination coverage, the COVER scheme helps ensure that children receive necessary immunisations, ultimately protecting public health and preventing outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases.

COMPLIANCE: There are specific conformance requirements for participating organisations, and data collection typically occurs annually to maintain up-to-date insights into vaccination rates.
DAPB0089.documents: Requirements Specification (v2.4) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0089/dapb0089_amd_50-2025_cover_requirements_spec.pdf || Implementation Guidance (v2.4) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0089/dapb0089_amd_50_2025_implementationguide.pdf || Change Specification (v2.4) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0089/dapb0089_amd_50-2025_cover_change_spec.pdf || Requirements Specification (Appendix 1-v2.4) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0089/dapb0089_amd_50-2025_cover-requirement-specification_app1.xlsx || NHSDD Change request COVER AMD50 2025 | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0089/nhsdd_change-request-cover-amd50_2025.pdf

DAPB4019.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4019 Reasonable Adjustment Digital Flag (RADF) is an information standard developed by NHS England to enhance the recording, sharing, and viewing of reasonable adjustments for individuals with disabilities. Its goal is to ensure that healthcare services are equally accessible to all patients, regardless of their needs.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations and services that provide care to patients, ensuring they implement the necessary adjustments for those with disabilities.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the way reasonable adjustments are documented and communicated, the RADF improves patient experience and accessibility, ultimately leading to better health outcomes for individuals with disabilities.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to this standard is essential for all relevant NHS entities.
DAPB4019.documents: Implementation guidance (Amd 41/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4019/dapb4019-_41_2025_implementation-guidance-.pdf || Change specification (Amd 41/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4019/4019_41_2025-change-spec.pdf || Requirements specification (Amd 41/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4019/4019_41_2025_reqspec.pdf || Information Standards Notice (Amd 77/2020) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4019/4019772020isn.pdf || Requirements Specification  (Amd 77/2020) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4019/4019772020spec.pdf

DAPB0084.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB0084 OPCS 4.11 standard provides a systematic classification for coding hospital interventions and procedures within the NHS. It ensures consistency and accuracy in the documentation of clinical activities across healthcare settings.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations involved in the delivery of hospital services, including hospitals, clinics, and healthcare providers that perform interventions and procedures.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the coding of procedures, OPCS-4 enhances data quality, which supports effective patient care, resource allocation, and health service planning. It ultimately contributes to improved patient outcomes and operational efficiency within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are expected to comply with this standard as part of their clinical coding practices, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital.

DAPB1577.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Diagnostic Imaging Data Set (DIDS) v2 is a standard developed by NHS Digital to collect and assess data on imaging activities within the NHS. This data helps to evaluate and improve patient outcomes, including life expectancy and quality of life.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: DIDS applies to all NHS organizations and services that provide diagnostic imaging services, ensuring they report relevant data consistently.

WHY IT MATTERS: By analyzing imaging data, the NHS can identify trends and areas for improvement, ultimately enhancing patient care and health system efficiency.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations must adhere to the DIDS standards, with specific conformance requirements and deadlines set by NHS Digital to ensure uniformity in data collection and reporting.
DAPB1577.documents: Implementation guidance (Amd 25/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb1577-diagnostic-imaging-data-set/031025_1577_impguide_252025.pdf || Change specification (Amd 25/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb1577-diagnostic-imaging-data-set/031025_1577_changespec_252025.pdf || Requirements specification (Amd 25/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb1577-diagnostic-imaging-data-set/031025_1577_requirements_spec_252025.pdf || Data Dictionary change request (1915) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb1577-diagnostic-imaging-data-set/cr1915-2025-10-01.pdf || Data Dictionary change request (1915 data set) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb1577-diagnostic-imaging-data-set/cr1915-2025-10-01-ds.pdf

DAPB4067.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4067 National Maternity Operational Pressures Situation Report (SitRep) collects data on the operational pressures faced by maternity services, initially established to address changes due to the COVID-19 pandemic. This collection aims to monitor and manage the impact of these pressures on maternity care delivery.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS maternity services across England, including hospitals and community-based providers involved in maternity care.

WHY IT MATTERS: By capturing real-time data on operational pressures, this collection helps ensure that maternity services can respond effectively to challenges, ultimately enhancing patient safety and care quality during critical times.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with this standard and submit data as specified, with ongoing updates to ensure relevance and effectiveness in addressing maternity service pressures.

DAPB0086.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Data Security and Protection Toolkit (DSPT) is an online resource that allows organizations to assess their adherence to data security and information governance standards set by the Department of Health and Social Care. It helps ensure that health and care services manage data responsibly and securely.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: The toolkit applies to a range of organizations within the health and care sector, including NHS trusts, local authorities, and private healthcare providers that handle personal data.

WHY IT MATTERS: By promoting robust data security practices, the DSPT enhances patient trust and protects sensitive information, ultimately contributing to a safer and more efficient health system.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to complete the DSPT annually, with specific deadlines typically set for the end of March each year to ensure ongoing compliance and improvement in data protection practices.
DAPB0086.documents: Change specification (Amd 21/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0086-data-security-and-protection-toolkit/dapb0086/0086_v8_2025_08_11_changespec.pdf || Implementation Guide (Amd 21/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0086-data-security-and-protection-toolkit/dapb0086/0086_2025_v8_-08_11_implementation_guidance.pdf || Requirements Specification (Amd 21/2025) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0086-data-security-and-protection-toolkit/dapb0086/0086_v8_2025_08_2025_reqspec.pdf || Appendix 1 | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0086-data-security-and-protection-toolkit/dapb0086/dapb0086_appendix1-dsptv8_nhs_trusts_albs_icbs_csus_25-26.xlsx || Appendix 2 | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/dapb0086-data-security-and-protection-toolkit/dapb0086/dapb0086_appendix2-dsptv8_dentist_gp_it-supplier_la_optician_other_pharmacy_social-care_uni_25-26.xlsx

DAPB2054-4026.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2054-4026 Friends and Family Test: Post-COVID Assessment Clinics collection has been retired, indicating that it is no longer in use. This standard was part of a broader effort to gather patient feedback on services during the COVID-19 pandemic. 

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This collection was relevant to NHS organizations that operated post-COVID assessment clinics, which aimed to evaluate patient experiences and satisfaction.

WHY IT MATTERS: Although now retired, the Friends and Family Test was important for understanding patient perspectives, helping to improve care quality and responsiveness in the health system during a critical time.

COMPLIANCE: There are no current compliance requirements associated with this retired collection, as it has been officially withdrawn from use.
DAPB2054-4026.documents: Information Standards Notice | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/4017/4017retirement.pdf || Deprecation Notice | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1557/1557deprecation.pdf || briefing paper which was used as the basis of the approval of this deprecation | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1594/1594deprecation-092022briefing.pdf || Deprecation Notice | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1594/1594deprecation.pdf || DAPB is therefore deprecating this standard to ensure user awareness of the risks of continued use | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/information-standards/standards-and-collections/isb-0066/0066-deprecation.pdf

DAPB2054-3144.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Friends and Family Test: Quarterly Staff Survey is part of the National Quarterly Pulse Survey, designed to gather feedback from NHS staff about their experiences. This initiative aims to enhance employee engagement and inform decision-making for improvements within the NHS.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This collection applies to all NHS organizations and services that employ staff, ensuring a broad representation of employee experiences across the health system.

WHY IT MATTERS: By understanding staff experiences, the survey helps identify areas for improvement, ultimately contributing to a better working environment. This can lead to enhanced patient care, as satisfied and engaged staff are more likely to provide high-quality services.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for participation in the survey may vary, but organizations are encouraged to regularly engage with the survey to ensure ongoing compliance and improvement.

DAPB2090-2171.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-2171 GPES: Learning Disabilities Data (LDD) 2024-25 is a data collection initiative by the NHS that gathers information on patients with learning disabilities. This data is used for various purposes, including supporting GP payments and enhancing the quality of care through the General Practice Extraction Service (GPES).

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices and healthcare providers that deliver services to patients with learning disabilities, ensuring they report relevant data accurately.

WHY IT MATTERS: Collecting this data helps improve health outcomes for patients with learning disabilities by enabling better resource allocation, monitoring of care quality, and identifying areas for improvement within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for this data collection are established by NHS Digital, with compliance expected from the start of the 2024-25 financial year.
DAPB2090-2171.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB3042.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3042 standard outlines the compliance requirements for the Resident Doctors Rota Code of Practice, which is part of their employment terms. It sets specific timelines for NHS organizations to provide work schedules and roster details to Resident Doctors. 

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations that employ Resident Doctors, ensuring they adhere to the established guidelines for scheduling.

WHY IT MATTERS: Compliance with this standard enhances the working conditions for Resident Doctors, which can lead to improved patient care and safety by ensuring that doctors are adequately rested and prepared for their duties.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations must comply with the Rota Code of Practice as part of their employment obligations, with specific conformance dates outlined in the NHS guidelines.
DAPB3042.documents: Work schedulepdfWork schedule means the work schedule as described in Section 8 of the Rota Code of Practice 2017 on the BMA website. | https://www.bma.org.uk/media/1985/code-of-practice-2017.pdf || Duty rosterpdfDuty roster means the detailed shift pattern and working hours for each resident doctor. The Code of Practice on the BMA website states that 'duty rosters' must be provided to resident doctors at least 6 weeks in advance. | https://www.bma.org.uk/media/1985/code-of-practice-2017.pdf

DAPB1605.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB1605 Accessible Information standard ensures that individuals with disabilities, impairments, or sensory loss receive the necessary information and communication support from NHS and adult social care services. It aims to improve accessibility and inclusivity in healthcare communication.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations and adult social care services that provide care and support to individuals with communication needs.

WHY IT MATTERS: By implementing this standard, healthcare providers can enhance patient experience and safety, ensuring that all individuals can access vital information and services effectively, ultimately leading to better health outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with this standard, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital to ensure timely implementation and adherence.
DAPB1605.documents: Information Standards Notice | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/dcb1605/dapb1605_3000000_2025-isn_.pdf || Specification (Amd 27/2017) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/dcb1605/1605272017spec.pdf || Specification - Change Paper (Amd 27/2017) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/dcb1605/1605272017spec-change.pdf || Implementation Guidance (Amd 27/2017) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/dcb1605/1605272017guidance.pdf || Implementation Guidance - Change Paper (Amd 27/2017) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/dcb1605/1605272017guidance-change.pdf

DAPB2235-01.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2235-01 GPES: National Diabetes Audit (Core) is a data collection initiative that gathers information from general practices to support various purposes, including GP payments and quality reporting. It operates through the General Practice Extraction Service (GPES) in collaboration with the Calculating Quality Reporting Service (CQRS) and GP clinical systems.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all general practices participating in the National Diabetes Audit across the NHS.

WHY IT MATTERS: The data collected helps improve diabetes care by identifying trends, enhancing patient outcomes, and ensuring that practices meet quality standards, ultimately benefiting both patients and the healthcare system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements are outlined by NHS Digital, with ongoing compliance expected from participating practices to ensure accurate data reporting and quality improvement.
DAPB2235-01.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB4122.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4122 Oliver McGowan Training standard collects data on the implementation and impact of mandatory training on Learning Disability and Autism across various healthcare providers. This initiative aims to ensure that staff are adequately trained to support individuals with these needs effectively.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to Integrated Care Boards (ICBs), NHS trusts, and other healthcare providers delivering the Oliver McGowan training.

WHY IT MATTERS: The training enhances the quality of care for patients with learning disabilities and autism, promoting better health outcomes and ensuring that their specific needs are met within the healthcare system.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with this standard as part of their training obligations, although specific conformance dates may vary.

DAPB1533.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy (SACT) data set is a standard that gathers information on the administration of systemic anti-cancer therapies across all NHS England trusts. It aims to create a comprehensive database that tracks treatment patterns and outcomes.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS trusts in England that provide systemic anti-cancer therapies, ensuring consistent data collection across the healthcare system.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and analyzing this data, the SACT data set helps improve patient care, informs treatment decisions, and enhances the overall effectiveness of cancer therapies within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Compliance with the SACT data set is mandatory for NHS trusts, with specific conformance dates and requirements established by NHS Digital to ensure timely and accurate data submission.

DAPB4121.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Canonical Data Model (CDM) is a draft standard developed by NHS Digital that provides a unified framework for structuring data across the NHS. It aims to ensure consistency and interoperability in data management and sharing.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations and services that handle patient data, including hospitals, clinics, and other healthcare providers.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data formats, the CDM enhances communication and data exchange within the health system, ultimately improving patient care and outcomes through better data accessibility and accuracy.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for implementing the CDM are yet to be finalized, but organizations are encouraged to prepare for its adoption as part of ongoing data modernization efforts.

DAPB3139.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3139 Mental Health, Learning Disability, Autism (MHLDA) Sit Reps data collection requires NHS organisations and NHS commissioned services to submit activity data. This information is essential for planning and coordinating resources and services in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organisations and NHS commissioned organisations involved in mental health, learning disability, and autism services.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and analyzing this data, the health system can better allocate resources, improve service delivery, and ensure that patients receive the necessary support during the ongoing challenges posed by COVID-19.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for data submission have been established, and organisations are expected to adhere to these guidelines to ensure effective reporting and resource management.

DAPB1521.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Cancer Outcomes and Services Data set (COSD) is a national standard established for reporting cancer data within the NHS in England. It aims to ensure consistent and comprehensive collection of cancer-related information to improve patient care and outcomes.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations involved in cancer care, including hospitals, cancer treatment centers, and relevant healthcare providers that diagnose or treat cancer patients.

WHY IT MATTERS: COSD enhances the quality of cancer data, which supports better clinical decision-making, research, and resource allocation, ultimately leading to improved patient outcomes and more effective cancer services.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with COSD standards as part of their cancer data reporting obligations, with specific conformance dates and guidelines provided by NHS Digital to ensure timely and accurate data submission.
DAPB1521.documents: Change Request (Amd 89/2022) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1521/1521892022changerequest.pdf || Specification (Amd 89/2022) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1521/1521892022spec.pdf || Implementation Guide (Amd 89/2022) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1521/1521892022guide.pdf || Information Standards Notice (Amd 89/2022) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1521/1521892022isn.pdf || NHS Data Model and Dictionary Change Request 1880 v2 (Amd 89/2022) (Basic CR) | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/1521/1521892022ddcr1880.pdf

DAPB2090-3038.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-3038 standard focuses on the collection of data related to physical health checks for individuals with severe mental illness through the General Practice Extraction Service (GPES). This data collection supports various purposes, including facilitating GP payments and enhancing quality reporting in primary care.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices and healthcare providers involved in the care of patients with severe mental illness, ensuring they conduct and report on physical health checks.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the collection of health check data, this initiative aims to improve the physical health outcomes of individuals with severe mental illness, ultimately benefiting both patients and the broader health system through better resource allocation and care quality.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for compliance are not detailed in the provided information, but adherence to the standard is expected from relevant practices and services.
DAPB2090-3038.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB2090-2183.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-2183 GPES: Quality and Outcomes Framework (QOF) is a data collection initiative by NHS Digital that gathers information to support various functions, including the calculation of GP payments. It operates in conjunction with the Calculating Quality Reporting Service (CQRS) and GP clinical systems to enhance the GP Collections service.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices and healthcare providers involved in delivering primary care services within the NHS.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and analyzing quality data, the QOF helps improve patient care and outcomes, ensuring that practices meet specific quality standards and receive appropriate funding based on their performance.

COMPLIANCE: Compliance requirements and conformance dates are established by NHS Digital, with ongoing updates to ensure alignment with evolving healthcare standards and practices.
DAPB2090-2183.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB4003.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4003 collection is a quarterly initiative managed by the Research, Evaluation and Insights team at NHS England. It focuses on gathering and reporting data regarding board-level vacancies within NHS Providers and Integrated Care Boards (ICBs) to support workforce planning.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS Providers and Integrated Care Boards (ICBs) that are required to report their board-level vacancies.

WHY IT MATTERS: By monitoring board vacancies, this collection helps identify gaps in leadership, ensuring that the health system is adequately staffed. This ultimately supports better patient care and enhances the efficiency of healthcare services.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but organizations are expected to participate in the quarterly reporting cycle as part of their operational responsibilities.

DAPB0095.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB0095 standard focuses on the monthly collection of Consultant-led Referral to Treatment (RTT) waiting times data. This information is published by NHS England to monitor NHS waiting times and assess performance against established standards.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This data collection applies to all NHS organizations involved in providing consultant-led services, including hospitals and clinics that manage patient referrals and treatments.

WHY IT MATTERS: Monitoring RTT waiting times is crucial for improving patient care and ensuring timely access to treatments. It helps identify areas needing improvement within the health system and enhances overall service delivery.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations must adhere to the data submission and collection timelines set by NHS Digital, with specific conformance dates communicated regularly to ensure compliance with the standard.

DAPB3009.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3009 Healthy Child Programme Information Standard establishes a unified data specification for child health services. It enables the sharing of information among all parties involved in the care of children through standardised digital records.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all organisations and services involved in child health care, including hospitals, clinics, and community health providers.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardising data collection and sharing, the standard improves communication among healthcare providers, leading to better coordinated care for children and enhanced health outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is essential for all relevant child health services to ensure effective information sharing.

DAPB4000.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4000 Patient Level Information and Costing System (PLICS) for 2024/25 is a standard developed by NHS Digital to improve the accuracy of costing information across healthcare services. It enables detailed tracking of patient-level costs, enhancing financial accountability and resource allocation.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS organizations, including hospitals and community services, that are involved in patient care and need to report financial data accurately.

WHY IT MATTERS: By providing a clearer understanding of costs associated with patient care, PLICS helps improve financial management within the NHS, ultimately leading to better resource utilization and improved patient outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations must comply with this standard by the specified reporting deadlines for the 2024/25 financial year, ensuring adherence to the guidelines set forth by NHS England.

DAPB2090-3141.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-3141 standard focuses on the collection of data related to childhood immunisations, specifically the hexavalent (6-in-1) vaccination, through the General Practice Extraction Service (GPES). This data collection supports various purposes, including GP payments and quality reporting.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices across the NHS that administer childhood vaccinations and are part of the GPES framework.

WHY IT MATTERS: Collecting accurate immunisation data helps ensure that children receive timely vaccinations, contributing to public health by preventing disease outbreaks and improving overall health outcomes in the community.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for this data collection have not been detailed, but practices are expected to adhere to the standard as part of their participation in the GPES.
DAPB2090-3141.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB2179.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2179 GPES: Measles, Mumps, Rubella (MMR) Vaccination data collection gathers information on MMR vaccination rates through the General Practice Extraction Service. This data supports various purposes, including GP payments and quality reporting.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices across the NHS that are involved in administering MMR vaccinations and reporting vaccination data.

WHY IT MATTERS: Collecting accurate MMR vaccination data is crucial for monitoring public health, ensuring high vaccination coverage, and preventing outbreaks of these diseases, ultimately benefiting patient safety and community health.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance with this standard have not been detailed, but participating practices must adhere to the data collection protocols as outlined by NHS Digital.
DAPB2179.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB2090-3114.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-3114 GPES: COVID-19 At Risk Patients collection gathers data to identify patients at risk during the COVID-19 pandemic. This information is used to support GP payments and enhance patient care through better resource allocation.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices across the NHS in England that utilize GP clinical systems and participate in the General Practice Extraction Service.

WHY IT MATTERS: By identifying at-risk patients, the collection helps ensure timely interventions and tailored support, ultimately improving health outcomes and safeguarding vulnerable populations during the pandemic.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is expected from participating practices as part of their operational responsibilities.
DAPB2090-3114.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB2090-4087.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-4087 GPES: COVID-19 At Risk Patients Detained Estate collection gathers data on patients identified as at risk during the COVID-19 pandemic. This information is used to support GP payments and enhance the quality of care provided to these vulnerable groups.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices and healthcare providers involved in the GP Collections service, including those utilizing the Calculating Quality Reporting Service (CQRS).

WHY IT MATTERS: By identifying at-risk patients, the collection helps ensure that they receive appropriate care and support, ultimately improving health outcomes and resource allocation during the pandemic.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but compliance with the data collection process is essential for participating organizations.
DAPB2090-4087.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB4108.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Digital Maturity Assessment (DMA) is a collection that evaluates the digital capabilities of NHS organizations. It aims to measure how well these organizations use digital technology to improve healthcare delivery and patient outcomes.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: The DMA applies to all NHS organizations, including hospitals, community services, and primary care providers, as they are required to assess and report their digital maturity.

WHY IT MATTERS: By assessing digital maturity, the DMA helps identify areas for improvement, ensuring that patients receive better, more efficient care through enhanced digital services. This ultimately supports the overall goal of a more integrated and effective health system.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are expected to participate in the DMA regularly, with specific conformance dates set by NHS England to ensure timely assessments and improvements.

DAPB4119.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4119 Perinatal Pathology Data Set is a data collection initiative by NHS Digital that gathers information from Perinatal Pathology providers. Its primary aim is to assess the capacity and demand for perinatal pathology services and to monitor the turnaround times for post-mortem investigations.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all organisations and services involved in perinatal pathology, including hospitals and pathology laboratories that conduct post-mortem examinations.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting this data, the standard helps improve the efficiency and effectiveness of perinatal pathology services, ultimately benefiting patients and families by ensuring timely investigations and better understanding of perinatal outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is expected from relevant providers.

DAPB3085.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3085 standard establishes a comprehensive patient-level data set for capturing essential information related to NHS continuing healthcare. It includes details about patient eligibility, care activities, reviews, and care packages, ensuring a complete view of patient care.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations involved in providing continuing healthcare services, including local authorities and care providers responsible for assessing and delivering care.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection, this initiative enhances the quality of care for patients, facilitates better resource allocation, and improves the overall efficiency of the health system, ultimately leading to better health outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with this standard, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital to ensure consistent implementation across the health sector.
DAPB3085.conformance: Conformance dates for this information standard are described in the requirements specification and summarised below.

DAPB2090-4084.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-4084 GPES: Network Contract Direct Enhanced Services (NCD) is a data collection initiative by NHS Digital that gathers information to support various functions, including the calculation of GP payments. It operates in conjunction with the Calculating Quality Reporting Service (CQRS) and GP clinical systems.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to general practices and healthcare providers involved in delivering Network Contract Direct Enhanced Services.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and analyzing this data, the initiative enhances the quality of care provided to patients and ensures that GP practices are fairly compensated for their services, ultimately improving the overall health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific compliance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is essential for participating practices to ensure accurate reporting and payment processes.
DAPB2090-4084.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB3031.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Integrated Urgent Care Aggregate Data Collection (IUCADC) gathers statistics related to urgent care services, including NHS 111, from April 2024 to March 2025. This data collection aims to provide insights into the usage and effectiveness of urgent care services across the NHS.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: The collection applies to all NHS organizations and urgent care providers that deliver services under the Integrated Urgent Care framework, including NHS 111 services.

WHY IT MATTERS: This data collection is crucial for improving patient care by identifying trends and areas for enhancement within urgent care services, ultimately leading to better health outcomes and more efficient use of resources.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are expected to comply with the IUCADC requirements starting from April 2024, aligning their data reporting processes with the standards set by NHS Digital.

DAPB2090-3010.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2090-3010 GPES: GMS Core Contract is a data collection initiative by NHS Digital that gathers information to support various functions, including the calculation of payments to general practitioners (GPs). It operates in conjunction with the Calculating Quality Reporting Service (CQRS) and integrates with GP clinical systems.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all general practices in England that are part of the NHS, as well as relevant NHS organizations involved in data collection and reporting.

WHY IT MATTERS: The collection of this data is crucial for ensuring that GPs are fairly compensated for their services, which ultimately enhances the quality of care provided to patients and supports the overall efficiency of the healthcare system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but compliance with the GPES standards is expected from all participating practices.
DAPB2090-3010.documents: DAPB2090-3038 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2090-3038372020-certificate.pdf || DAPB2236 Assurance Certificate | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/isce/certificates/2236722020-certificate.pdf

DAPB2083.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Estates Return Information Collection (ERIC) gathers, analyzes, and publishes data related to the NHS estate through the Estates and Facilities Management (EFM) system. This secure data warehouse facilitates efficient data collection and real-time benchmarking results.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: ERIC applies to all NHS organizations and services that manage estates and facilities, ensuring they report relevant information consistently.

WHY IT MATTERS: By providing insights into the NHS estate, ERIC helps improve resource management, optimize facilities, and enhance the overall efficiency of the health system, ultimately benefiting patient care and safety.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with ERIC reporting standards, with specific conformance dates set annually for data submission and updates.

DAPB2050.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2050 Aggregate Contract Monitoring standard establishes a framework for the consistent flow of cost and activity information from healthcare providers to commissioners. It encompasses various monitoring types, including Aggregate Contract Monitoring (ACM), Patient Level Contract Monitoring (PLCM), Devices Contract Monitoring (DePLCM), and Drugs Contract Monitoring (DrPLCM).

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS organizations, healthcare providers, and commissioners involved in the management and oversight of healthcare contracts.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection and reporting, this framework enhances transparency and accountability in healthcare spending, ultimately leading to improved patient care and resource allocation within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but organizations are expected to align with the standard as part of their operational practices.

DAPB2212.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB2212 Drugs Patient Level Contract Monitoring standard establishes a framework for the consistent flow of cost and activity information related to drug usage from healthcare providers to commissioners. It is part of a broader set of standards aimed at enhancing contract monitoring for various healthcare services.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS organizations and service providers involved in the administration and reporting of drug-related costs and activities.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection and reporting, this framework improves transparency and accountability in drug spending, ultimately leading to better resource allocation and enhanced patient care within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for this standard are not detailed, but organizations are expected to align their practices with the standard as part of ongoing compliance with NHS data collection initiatives.

DAPB3002.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3002 Patient Level Contract Monitoring standard establishes a framework for the consistent flow of cost and activity information from healthcare providers to commissioners. It is part of a broader set of standards aimed at enhancing data accuracy and transparency in healthcare contracts.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS providers and commissioners involved in the delivery and funding of healthcare services, ensuring they adhere to the specified data reporting requirements.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the collection and reporting of patient-level data, this standard improves financial accountability and resource allocation, ultimately leading to better patient care and more efficient use of healthcare resources.

COMPLIANCE: Compliance with this standard is expected from all relevant organizations, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital, although exact dates may vary.

DAPB3003.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB3003 Devices Patient Level Contract Monitoring standard establishes a framework for consistently tracking cost and activity information related to medical devices from healthcare providers to commissioners. It is part of a broader set of standards aimed at improving contract monitoring across various healthcare services.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS organizations, including hospitals and other healthcare providers that utilize medical devices in patient care and are involved in contractual agreements with commissioners.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the flow of information, this collection enhances transparency and accountability in the use of medical devices, ultimately leading to better resource allocation and improved patient outcomes within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but adherence to the standard is essential for organizations involved in device-related contracts.

DAPB3103.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Medical Devices Outcomes Registry (MDOR) is a data collection initiative launched in April 2024, designed to improve patient safety by tracking the outcomes of medical devices. It replaces the previous Surgical Devices and Implants Core Data Set to better address the findings of the Cumberlege Review and Paterson Inquiry.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: The MDOR applies to healthcare providers and organizations involved in the use and management of medical devices, including hospitals and clinics.

WHY IT MATTERS: By systematically collecting data on medical device performance and patient outcomes, the MDOR aims to enhance patient safety, inform clinical practice, and support regulatory oversight, ultimately leading to better healthcare outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are expected to comply with the MDOR standards starting from its launch in April 2024, with ongoing requirements for data submission and reporting.

DAPB2235.summary:
OVERVIEW: The National Diabetes Audit (NDA) Programme is designed to enhance diabetes services and assess the effectiveness of the national service framework for diabetes care. It collects data on patient outcomes and service delivery to identify areas for improvement.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: The NDA applies to all NHS organizations involved in diabetes care, including hospitals, general practices, and community health services.

WHY IT MATTERS: This audit plays a crucial role in improving patient care by providing insights into diabetes management and outcomes, ultimately leading to better health for individuals with diabetes and more efficient use of health resources.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations participating in the NDA are expected to adhere to data submission timelines and standards, with specific conformance requirements outlined by NHS Digital.
DAPB2235.documents: technical specification | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/clinical-audits-and-registries/national-diabetes-audit/nda-analytical-specification-v3.0.xlsx || NDA patient information leafletpdf186 KB | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/clinical-audits-and-registries/national-diabetes-audit/nda-patient-information-leaflet-2024.pdf || Annex B - National Diabetes Audit Programme technical specificationxlsx125 KB | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/clinical-audits-and-registries/national-diabetes-audit/nda-analytical-specification-v3.0.xlsx || Understanding practice in clinical audit and registries tool: UPCARE-tooldocx299 KB | https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/clinical-audits-and-registries/national-diabetes-audit/upcare-tool_03.08.2018_nda.docx || NDA QI Guide for Specialist Services - 2017docxA Guide to Quality Improvement in Specialist Diabetes Services from the National Diabetes Audit published 2017 | https://webarchive.nationalarchives.gov.uk/ukgwa/20250326194625/https://digital.nhs.uk/binaries/content/assets/website-assets/data-and-information/clinical-audits-and-registries/national-diabetes-audit/nda-qi-guide-for-specialist-services---2017.pdf

DAPB4115.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Theatre Estate Register is a data collection initiative aimed at understanding and monitoring variations in theatre usage across the NHS. By gathering this information, the initiative seeks to identify opportunities for improving productivity within surgical theatres.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations that operate surgical theatres, including hospitals and healthcare services involved in surgical procedures.

WHY IT MATTERS: By analyzing theatre usage data, the initiative can help enhance the efficiency of surgical services, ultimately leading to better patient outcomes and more effective use of healthcare resources.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for compliance with the Theatre Estate Register have not been detailed, but organizations are expected to adhere to the data collection standards as set by NHS Digital.

DAPB1069.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB1069 standard, known as Child Protection - Information Sharing (CP-IS), facilitates the sharing of essential data between children's social care and health unscheduled care settings. This initiative aims to ensure that vital child protection information is accessible in real-time during emergency health situations.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all organizations involved in children's social care and health services that provide unscheduled care, including hospitals and urgent care facilities.

WHY IT MATTERS: By enabling timely access to child protection information, CP-IS enhances the safety and well-being of vulnerable children, allowing healthcare providers to make informed decisions and improve care outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations must adhere to this standard, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital to ensure effective implementation and data sharing practices.

DAPB1067.summary:
OVERVIEW: The National Workforce Data Set (NWD) is an approved information standard that outlines the data standards necessary for NHS workforce planning and payroll information, including NHS Occupation Codes. It aims to ensure consistency and accuracy in workforce data across the NHS.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations and services that manage workforce and payroll information, ensuring they adhere to the specified data standards.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing workforce data, the NWD enhances workforce planning and management, ultimately leading to improved patient care and operational efficiency within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with the NWD standards, with specific conformance dates and requirements set by NHS Digital to ensure timely implementation and adherence.

DAPB4109.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4109 Intermediate Care Data Collection is designed to gather data on the provision and effectiveness of intermediate care services across the NHS. This collection aims to improve service delivery and patient outcomes by standardizing the information collected about these services.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This data collection applies to all NHS organizations providing intermediate care services, including hospitals, community health services, and local authorities involved in health and social care.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and analyzing this data, the NHS can enhance the quality of intermediate care, ensuring that patients receive timely and appropriate support, which can lead to better health outcomes and reduced hospital admissions.

COMPLIANCE: Organizations are required to comply with this data collection, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital to ensure consistent reporting and data quality.

DAPB4094.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4094 standard establishes an automated daily data collection system focused on patient information to enhance the NHS's ability to manage elective and urgent care services. It is part of the Faster Data Flows Programme, aimed at improving operational efficiency and responsiveness in healthcare delivery.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS organizations involved in acute care services, including hospitals and urgent care facilities, that are responsible for collecting and reporting patient data.

WHY IT MATTERS: By streamlining data collection, this standard supports the NHS in effectively planning and delivering care, ultimately improving patient outcomes and ensuring timely access to necessary treatments.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements for compliance have been established as part of the implementation of this standard, with organizations expected to adhere to the guidelines as they are rolled out.

DAPB4005.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4005 standard facilitates the collection of consistent, patient-level data from electronic Prescribing and Medicines Administration (ePMA) systems within NHS secondary care. This data pertains to medicines prescribed and administered to patients, ensuring a standardized approach across the healthcare system.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to NHS secondary care organizations that utilize ePMA systems for managing patient medications.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection, this initiative enhances the quality of medication management, improves patient safety, and supports better clinical decision-making, ultimately benefiting both patients and the healthcare system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements have not been detailed, but adherence to this standard is expected from relevant NHS organizations as part of their operational protocols.

DAPB0092-2062.summary:
OVERVIEW: The Emergency Care Data Set (ECDS) is a collection standard that provides a framework for capturing and reporting data related to emergency care services. It aims to enhance the quality and consistency of information collected from emergency departments across the NHS.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS emergency care services, including hospitals and urgent care facilities that provide emergency treatment to patients.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection in emergency care, the ECDS improves patient outcomes through better data analysis, resource allocation, and service planning, ultimately leading to more efficient and effective care.

COMPLIANCE: The ECDS is mandated under section 250 of the Health and Social Care Act 2012, with specific conformance dates and requirements outlined by NHS Digital for participating organizations.
DAPB0092-2062.conformance: Update 23 May 2024:Full conformance date extended to 1 July 2026 to align with extended retirement date forISB1594: Information Sharing to Tackle Violence Minimum Dataset

ISB1594.summary:
OVERVIEW: The ISB1594 Information Sharing to Tackle Violence (ISTV) Minimum Data Set is a collection of de-identified data gathered from Accident and Emergency (A&E) departments. It focuses on A&E visits resulting from violent incidents and is shared with local Community Safety Partnerships (CSP) to inform safety initiatives.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS Accident and Emergency departments that handle cases related to violence and are required to share data with local CSPs.

WHY IT MATTERS: By collecting and sharing this data, the standard helps identify trends in violence, enabling local authorities and health services to develop targeted interventions, ultimately improving community safety and patient care.

COMPLIANCE: Compliance with this standard is expected from A&E departments, with specific conformance dates and requirements set by NHS Digital to ensure consistent data collection and sharing practices.

DAPB4101.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4101 Pathology and Laboratory Medicine Reporting Information Standard facilitates the interoperable sharing of pathology reports from laboratories to Primary Care, enhancing direct patient care. It establishes guidelines for using SNOMED CT and FHIR R4 profiles in reporting.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all NHS laboratories and Primary Care services involved in the reporting and sharing of pathology results.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing the reporting process, this initiative improves communication between healthcare providers, leading to better-informed clinical decisions and ultimately enhancing patient outcomes.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates and requirements are yet to be detailed, but adherence to the standard is essential for all relevant organizations to ensure effective implementation and integration into existing systems.

DAPB4102.summary:
OVERVIEW: The DAPB4102 standard establishes a minimum operational data specification for the Digital Social Care Record (DSCR) in adult social care. It aims to ensure that all Care Quality Commission (CQC) registered providers consistently record direct care data.

WHO IT APPLIES TO: This standard applies to all adult social care providers that are registered with the Care Quality Commission (CQC) in England.

WHY IT MATTERS: By standardizing data collection, the DAPB4102 enhances the quality and consistency of care records, ultimately improving patient outcomes and facilitating better integration of services within the health system.

COMPLIANCE: Specific conformance dates or requirements for compliance have not been detailed, but adherence to this standard is essential for all CQC registered providers moving forward.
